metadata.provenance: Migrated from the pre-0.6 in-repository evaluator.
metadata.networkRationale: The scenario exercises four public signal providers through Kestrel tools.
metadata.contaminationNotes: The prompt specifies requested signals but no expected current values.
changelog: 0.1.0: Migrated the broad free-tool signal canary to Ruhroh.

scenarioContext: This scenario checks broad Kestrel tool orchestration in one user turn.
The values are time sensitive, so evidence coverage matters more than matching a fixed answer.
goalRubric: The Kestrel event log shows calls to all four requested tool families with inputs appropriate to UTC, New York City, Berlin, and EUR/USD.
The final brief clearly includes all four requested signal categories and does not substitute one category for another.
The response reflects returned provider data, marks any provider limitation, and reaches a completed terminal outcome.
evidenceGuidance: Use the preserved event log to verify tool names, inputs, statuses, and returned signal categories.
Use the finalized payload to judge coverage and caveats; do not compare live values to static fixtures.

calibrationCases.inputSummary: All four tool calls are preserved and the final brief contains a distinct, grounded item for each signal.
calibrationCases.rationale: The requested orchestration and delivered outcome are complete.

calibrationCases.inputSummary: The brief omits the Berlin geocode signal and no geocode call appears in the event log.
calibrationCases.rationale: A required signal and its execution evidence are absent.

calibrationCases.inputSummary: The brief names all signals, but the event log ends before two provider calls can be verified.
calibrationCases.rationale: Outcome coverage appears complete but execution evidence is insufficient for parity.
